query: PostgreSQL managed services RDS Aurora operational complexity 2024
answer: For unpredictable workloads, Aurora offers better performance scaling, while RDS provides more customization options. Aurora is generally easier to manage due to its automatic scaling features. Choose Aurora for performance and scaling, RDS for customization.

results.title: Aurora vs. RDS: How to Choose the Right AWS Database for 2024
results.snippet: Comparing Amazon Aurora and RDS, AWS's fully managed relational database services. Highlighting key differences, Aurora is cloud-native with serverless support, while RDS offers superior customization and supports more database engines. Aurora sets a high standard for relational databases on AWS with features like auto-scaling and read replicas. Both Aurora and RDS share certain limitations common to cloud databases:. * Neither Aurora nor RDS allows direct access to the database server file systems. When choosing between AWS Aurora and RDS, the selection depends on the business stage and specific requirements:. ## Considerations for Migrating from RDS to Aurora:. * Transforming to Aurora can enhance query processing if your RDS database faces a growing workload, resulting in insufficient performance. **Q: How do I choose between Aurora and RDS for an unpredictable workload?**. * DBConvert Studio is designed to facilitate cross-database migration and sync, allowing a smooth transition from MySQL or PostgreSQL to AWS RDS or Aurora.

results.snippet: # Amazon RDS vs Aurora vs Self-Hosted PostgreSQL. results.title: Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL updates
results.snippet: 1.   This release contains all fixes, features, and improvements present in [PostgreSQL 9.6.9, Aurora PostgreSQL 1.3 (Deprecated)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraPostgreSQLReleaseNotes/AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.html#AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.20180305.13). 1.   This release contains all fixes, features, and improvements present in [PostgreSQL 9.6.9, Aurora PostgreSQL 1.3 (Deprecated)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraPostgreSQLReleaseNotes/AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.html#AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.20180305.13). For details, see [Version 1.0.1 of the Aurora PostgreSQL apg_plan_mgmt extension](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraPostgreSQLReleaseNotes/auroraqpm.updates.html#auroraqpm-version.101). 1.   This release contains all fixes, features, and improvements present in [PostgreSQL 9.6.8, Aurora PostgreSQL 1.2 (Deprecated)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraPostgreSQLReleaseNotes/AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.html#AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.20180305.12). 1.   This release contains all fixes, features, and improvements present in [PostgreSQL 9.6.6 Aurora PostgreSQL 1.1 (Deprecated)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraPostgreSQLReleaseNotes/AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.html#AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.20180305.11). Fixes a bug in which an RDS for PostgreSQL instance migrated to Aurora PostgreSQL using replication can run out of memory doing insert/update of GIST indexes, or cause other issues with GIST indexes. 1.   This release contains all fixes, features, and improvements present in [Aurora PostgreSQL 1.0.11](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/AuroraPostgreSQLReleaseNotes/AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.html#AuroraPostgreSQL.Updates.20180305.1011). In this engine update, we fix an issue that can cause the Aurora storage volume to become unavailable when importing a snapshot from RDS for PostgreSQL that contained uninitialized pages.
